     The Numbers Business Report

War Dogs (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $43,034,523Details
International Box Office $43,400,000
Worldwide Box Office $86,434,523
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,189,591
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,208,440
Total $6,398,031
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Follows two friends in their early 20s living in Miami Beach during the Iraq War who exploit a little-known government initiative that allows small businesses to bid on U.S. Military contracts. Starting small, they begin raking in big money and are living the high life. But the pair gets in over their heads when they land a 300 million dollar deal to arm the Afghan Military—a deal that puts them in business with some very shady people, not the least of which turns out to be the U.S. Government.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,685,305 (34.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.93 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,258 opening theaters/3,258 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $59,502,064

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 19th, 2016 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Australia)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide), released as Týpci a zbraně (Czech Republic)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
August 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: November 11th, 2016 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, drug use and some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2396 (Cert #50097), 10/21/2015)
Running Time: 114 minutes
Plot point: War Profiteers
Social setting: Afghanistan War
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Joint Effort, The Mark Gordon Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Follows two friends in their early 20s living in Miami Beach during the Iraq War who exploit a little-known government initiative that allows small businesses to bid on U.S. Military contracts. Starting small, they begin raking in big money and are living the high life. But the pair gets in over their heads when they land a 300 million dollar deal to arm the Afghan Military—a deal that puts them in business with some very shady people, not the least of which turns out to be the U.S. Government.
